bibliographic_data.title: AudAgent: Automated Auditing of Privacy Policy Compliance in AI Agents
bibliographic_data.abstract: AI agents can autonomously perform tasks and, often without explicit user consent, collect or disclose users’ sensitive local data, which raises serious privacy concerns. Although AI agents’ privacy policies describe their intended data practices, there remains limited transparency and accountability about whether runtime behavior matches those policies. To bridge this gap, we present AudAgent, a tool that continuously monitors AI agents’ data practices in real time and guards compliance with their stated privacy policies.

core_content.novelty_claim: AudAgent introduces a novel cross-LLM voting mechanism for policy formalization, a lightweight Presidio-based analyzer for runtime annotation, ontology graphs and automata-based checking for compliance auditing, and an infrastructure-independent user interface.
core_content.key_methodology: AudAgent comprises four components: (i) Policy formalization using a cross-LLM voting mechanism; (ii) Runtime annotation with a lightweight Presidio-based analyzer; (iii) Compliance auditing through ontology graphs and automata-based checking; (iv) A user-friendly, infrastructure-independent interface.
outcomes: Automated extraction of privacy policy models from natural language documents
Real-time monitoring and annotation of sensitive data practices
On-the-fly compliance verification linking runtime annotations with formalized policies
User-friendly visualization of real-time execution traces and detected violations
core_content.full_text_markdown:
AI agents can autonomously perform tasks and, often without explicit user consent, collect or disclose users’ sensitive local data, which raises serious privacy concerns. Although AI agents’ privacy policies describe their intended data practices, there remains limited transparency and accountability about whether runtime behavior matches those policies. To bridge this gap, we present AudAgent, a tool that continuously monitors AI agents’ data practices in real time and guards compliance with their stated privacy policies.

AudAgent comprises four components for automated privacy auditing of AI agents: (i) Policy formalization using a novel cross-LLM voting mechanism; (ii) Runtime annotation with a lightweight Presidio-based analyzer; (iii) Compliance auditing through ontology graphs and automata-based checking; (iv) A user-friendly, infrastructure-independent interface that visualizes the real-time execution trace of AI agents alongside detected privacy violations.

We evaluate AudAgent on AI agents built with mainstream frameworks, demonstrating its effectiveness in detecting and visualizing privacy policy violations. Using AudAgent, we further find that many privacy policies lack explicit safeguards for highly sensitive data such as SSNs, whose misuse violates legal requirements, and that many agents—including those powered by Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek—do not refuse to process such data via third-party tools. AudAgent proactively blocks operations on such data, overriding the agents’ original privacy policies and behavior.

These results highlight that AudAgent complements existing AI agents with local privacy protection, enabling trustworthy deployments.
